Market Context: BEARISH — VIX 18.95 (+3.10%). Elevated vol regime. Signal: BEARISH (bear 3.0/5). SPX futures 7410. Pre-CPI tension. Oil $98.88. Breadth divergence — 52% above 50-day MA despite ATH.
Lessons Learned: Thin premium trade — $0.40 credit on 10 contracts. 2x stop triggered correctly at $0.80 but the thin premium meant the stop was too sensitive to gamma noise. SPX never breached the strike. Identified minimum credit floor as a candidate rule (~$0.60 for SPX).
Mistakes: NONE - thin premium identified as learning point. Stop functioned correctly.
Tags: Bull Put Spread, SPX, 0DTE, Live Account, Stop-out, Thin Premium

Market Context: BEARISH — VIX 18.95 (+3.10%). Elevated vol regime. Signal: BEARISH (bear 3.0/5). SPX futures 7410. Pre-CPI tension. Oil $98.88. Breadth divergence — 52% above 50-day MA despite ATH.
Lessons Learned: Low conviction trade with indecision. Opened @ $0.20, stopped out @ $0.55, re-entered @ $0.60, closed @ $0.25 for breakeven net. Four transactions generated $91.46 in fees/commissions for zero P&L. Trade indecision is expensive even when P&L is flat.

Market Context: BEARISH — VIX 18.70 (+1.74%). Elevated vol. CPI hot: 3.8% YoY headline, 2.8% core (both above expectations). SPX sold off to 7338 then recovered to 7366. Energy-driven inflation bleeding into shelter/services.
Lessons Learned: Post-CPI bear call spread. Thesis correct — SPX closed at 7366, well below 7395 strike. Stopped out on gamma noise from thin $0.35 premium. 2x stop at $0.70 triggered by a 15-point SPX move that never threatened the strike. Oversized at 15 contracts to compensate for thin premium — compounded the problem.
Mistakes: NONE - thin premium and oversizing identified as learning points. Stop functioned correctly.
Tags: Bear Call Spread, SPX, 0DTE, Live Account, Stop-out, Thin Premium, Oversized

Market Context: BEARISH — VIX 18.70 (+1.74%). Elevated vol. CPI hot: 3.8% YoY headline, 2.8% core (both above expectations). SPX sold off to 7338 then recovered to 7366. Energy-driven inflation bleeding into shelter/services.
Lessons Learned: Reopened same position after stop-out, driven by frustration not thesis. Recognised the rule breach (same-market-after-loss gate, Rule 10) and closed quickly. The fast self-correction is progress — but the reopen should never have happened. SPX finished at 7366; position would have been full profit if held.
Mistakes: NONE - rule breach. Rule 10 (same-market-after-loss gate) violated. Emotional reopen.
Tags: Bear Call Spread, SPX, 0DTE, Live Account, Rule Breach, Emotional Reopen

Market Context: BEARISH — VIX rising. PPI scorching hot: 1.4% MoM (vs 0.5% expected), 6.0% YoY. Core PPI 1.0% (vs 0.4% expected). Largest monthly PPI gain since March 2022. Rate hike odds climbed to 39%. SPX gapped down on open then recovered to new ATH 7438.
Lessons Learned: Unplanned entry — pre-market limit order from previous day's thesis filled at market open into hot PPI print. No plan for this trade existed. Cancelled stop under pressure when market chopped, then panic-closed at -$750 on a bounce. SPX recovered to 7438 — position would have expired worthless for full $700 profit. Cascade of errors from one operational mistake. Candidate Rule 19: unplanned entries get closed at market, no exceptions.
Mistakes: ERROR - Failed to cancel pre-market limit order. Unintended entry into PPI binary event. Cancelled stop under pressure. Panic close on bounce.
Tags: Bull Put Spread, SPX, 0DTE, Live Account, Unplanned Entry, Stop Cancelled, Panic Close
